title: pouteria aurea
intro: Pouteria aurea, also known as the golden guava, is a tropical plant species native to Central and South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other compounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and aiding in the management of diabetes. Herbal preparations of Pouteria aurea can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to take advantage of the plant's medicinal properties.
